entity.name: Sam Altman
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Advocates for targeted regulation of AI systems above certain capability thresholds, including licensing or registration requirements for development and release of advanced AI models. Testified before Senate in May 2023 supporting safety requirements, external testing, and publication of evaluation results. Has called for establishment of a global regulatory body modeled after the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) to manage AI risks, particularly around biosecurity threats. Recently negotiated Pentagon contract in March 2026 with specific safeguards against domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons, stating OpenAI would 'never do mass domestic surveillance, even if the government said it was legal, because it violates the constitution.' Initially signed 'opportunistic and sloppy' Pentagon deal but amended it after backlash to explicitly prohibit domestic surveillance of U.S. persons and require follow-on modifications for intelligence agency use. Acknowledges that while OpenAI can design safety guardrails, 'operational decisions' ultimately rest with government agencies like the Department of Defense.

claims.citation: The very rough way I try to think about it is when an AI system can do what very skilled humans in important jobs can do—I'd call that AGI. if you could hire an AI as a remote employee to be a great software engineer, I think a lot of people would say, 'OK, that's AGI-ish.'
claims.source_title: Sam Altman Interview: OpenAI CEO's Plans for ChatGPT, Fusion and Trump
claims.cached_excerpt: I still expect that on cybersecurity and bio stuff, we'll see serious, or potentially serious, short-term issues that need mitigation.

claims.citation: All of that said, to me, a system that can either autonomously discover new science or be such an incredible tool to people that our rate of scientific discovery in the world like quadruples or something.
claims.source_title: Sam Altman's Timeline for Artificial General Intelligence
claims.cached_excerpt: to me, a system that can either autonomously discover new science or be such an incredible tool to people that our rate of scientific discovery in the world like quadruples or something that would satisfy any test I could imagine for an AGI

claims.citation: AGI, short for "artificial general intelligence," is a nebulous term that OpenAI typically defines as "highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work."
claims.source_title: Sam Altman says "we are now confident we know how to build AGI" - Ars Technica
claims.cached_excerpt: AGI, short for "artificial general intelligence," is a nebulous term that OpenAI typically defines as "highly autonomous systems that outperform humans at most economically valuable work."

claims.citation: The thing that matters is the rate of progress that we have seen year-over-year over the last 5 years should continue for at least the next five probably well beyond that but hard to say.
claims.source_title: Sam Altman's Timeline for Artificial General Intelligence
claims.cached_excerpt: to me, a system that can either autonomously discover new science or be such an incredible tool to people that our rate of scientific discovery in the world like quadruples or something that would satisfy any test I could imagine for an AGI

claims.citation: If we are right, by the end of 2028, more of the world's intellectual capacity could reside inside of data centers than outside of them.
claims.source_title: AGI by 2028? Sam Altman Just Changed the Timeline - YouTube
claims.cached_excerpt: On our current trajectory, we believe we may be only a couple of years away from early versions of true superintelligence.

claims.citation: I still expect that on cybersecurity and bio stuff, we'll see serious, or potentially serious, short-term issues that need mitigation.
claims.source_title: Sam Altman Interview: OpenAI CEO's Plans for ChatGPT, Fusion and Trump
claims.cached_excerpt: I still expect that on cybersecurity and bio stuff, we'll see serious, or potentially serious, short-term issues that need mitigation.

claims.citation: Some people in the AI field think the risks of AGI (and successor systems) are fictitious. We would be delighted if they turn out to be right, but we are going to operate as if these risks are existential.
claims.source_title: How OpenAI's Sam Altman is keeping up the AI safety balancing act | Vox
claims.cached_excerpt: Some people in the AI field think the risks of AGI (and successor systems) are fictitious. We would be delighted if they turn out to be right, but we are going to operate as if these risks are existential.

claims.citation: Altman suggested that a catastrophic cyberattack or a biosecurity breach could occur as early as this year, necessitating a level of coordination between the U.S. Government, private tech firms, and international security agencies that does not yet exist.
claims.source_title: Sam Altman Warns U.S. Policymakers to Act Urgently on AI Risks
claims.cached_excerpt: Altman suggested that a catastrophic cyberattack or a biosecurity breach could occur as early as this year, necessitating a level of coordination between the U.S. Government, private tech firms, and international security agencies that does not yet exist.

claims.citation: Sam Altman, CEO of ChatGPT-maker OpenAI, warned at a Senate hearing Thursday that requiring government approval to release powerful artificial intelligence software would be "disastrous" for the United States' lead in the technology.
claims.source_title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's Senate testimony shows industry shift on regulation - The Washington Post
claims.cached_excerpt: Sam Altman, CEO of ChatGPT-maker OpenAI, warned at a Senate hearing Thursday that requiring government approval to release powerful artificial intelligence software would be 'disastrous' for the United States' lead in the technology.

claims.citation: One idea that's quite compelling, is something like an IAEA for advanced AI systems, for example, if you have 1 of the 10 systems in the world that is over this threshold – you're going to have the equivalent of weapons and specters and we're going to focus the international policy on catastrophic risk that affects all of us.
claims.source_title: An Interview with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman - HKS Student Policy Review
claims.cached_excerpt: AI policy works best downstream from the science, suggesting that policymaking should follow and adapt to the scientific developments in AI, rather than attempting to preemptively regulate the technology.

entity.name: Elon Musk
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Takes contradictory positions on AI regulation depending on context and competitive dynamics. Has called for AI regulation and pausing development when competitors were ahead, warning of catastrophic risks and advocating for government oversight. However, when his own xAI became competitive, shifted toward opposing restrictions. Federal government adopted xAI's Grok despite it violating the Trump Administration's own Executive Order 14319 requiring 'truth-seeking, accurate, and ideologically neutral' AI systems. Grok has been documented producing racist, antisemitic, and conspiratorial content, leading to California AG investigation and cease-and-desist orders over nonconsensual sexual deepfakes.
entity.belief_threat_models: Power concentration, Democratic erosion, Loss of control

claims.citation: Musk's complaint describes it as 'a general purpose artificial intelligence system—a machine having intelligence for a wide variety of tasks like a human.'
claims.source_title: Artificial general intelligence may already be obsolete as a concept
claims.cached_excerpt: His complaint describes it as "a general purpose artificial intelligence system—a machine having intelligence for a wide variety of tasks like a human."

claims.citation: In a post on X early Monday morning, Musk replied to X user Bojan Tunguz, who had written that he had "a fact based feeling that we'll have the full AGI by the end of this year." Musk's response was characteristically brief: "Feels like it."

claims.citation: I think what's running on the AI front, we are close to what you might call AGI, or digital superintelligence. I think we are seeing an explosion in digital superintelligence here.
claims.source_title: Elon Musk Says Control Over Tesla Matters More Than Compensation
claims.cached_excerpt: What I'm saying is that there should be some referees on the field, a few referees, but you shouldn't have a field jam-packed with referees, such that you could not kick a ball in any direction without hitting one.

claims.citation: I am very close to the cutting edge in AI and it scares the hell out of me. By the time we are reactive with AI regulation it is too late. AI is a fundamental existential risk for humanity.
claims.source_title: Elon Musk on AI Existential Risk: Why He Says Time Is Running Out
claims.cached_excerpt: I am very close to the cutting edge in AI and it scares the hell out of me. By the time we are reactive with AI regulation it is too late. AI is a fundamental existential risk for humanity.

claims.citation: I think there's some chance that it will end humanity. I probably agree with Geoff Hinton that it's about 10% or 20% or something like that.
claims.source_title: Elon Musk Says There Could Be a 20% Chance AI Destroys Humanity - Business Insider
claims.cached_excerpt: I think there's some chance that it will end humanity. I probably agree with Geoff Hinton that it's about 10% or 20% or something like that.

claims.citation: Government regulation that is applied at the state level in a patchwork across the country can have the effect to hamper innovation and deter competition in an open market.
claims.source_title: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over state's new AI law
claims.cached_excerpt: Government regulation that is applied at the state level in a patchwork across the country can have the effect to hamper innovation and deter competition in an open market.

claims.citation: So the fields that have been around for a long time, such as automotive, aerospace, you know, the sort of food and drug industries, are overregulated. But the new fields, like artificial intelligence, are under-regulated. In fact, there is no regulator at all.
claims.source_title: Elon Musk Says Control Over Tesla Matters More Than Compensation
claims.cached_excerpt: What I'm saying is that there should be some referees on the field, a few referees, but you shouldn't have a field jam-packed with referees, such that you could not kick a ball in any direction without hitting one.

entity.name: Yann LeCun
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Strong advocate for open-source AI development and minimal regulation that could impede research progress. Co-signed open letter to EU policymakers in 2024 criticizing fragmented regulatory decision-making and arguing that regulations should not impair open research, model training, and responsible product deployment. In September 2023 Senate Intelligence Committee testimony, supported voluntary commitments like the White House's AI commitments and collaborative industry standards rather than restrictive government oversight. Argues that open-source platforms are essential for preventing concentration of AI power and ensuring diverse, democratically-aligned AI systems. Called current AI systems 'not that smart' and argued against premature regulation of LLMs, stating in TIME interview that claims about needing to regulate LLMs because they're 'gonna be so dangerous' are 'just not true.

claims.citation: I hate the term. They're useful, there's no question. But they are not a path towards human-level intelligence.
claims.source_title: Meta's AI Chief Yann LeCun on AGI, Open-Source, and AI Risk
claims.cached_excerpt: I hate the term. They're useful, there's no question. But they are not a path towards human-level intelligence.

claims.citation: I don't like to call it AGI because human intelligence is not general at all. There are characteristics that intelligent beings have that no AI systems have today, like understanding the physical world; planning a sequence of actions to reach a goal; reasoning in ways that can take you a long time.
claims.source_title: Meta's AI Chief Yann LeCun on AGI, Open-Source, and AI Risk | TIME
claims.cached_excerpt: There's a number of fallacies there. The first fallacy is that because a system is intelligent, it wants to take control. That's just completely false.

claims.citation: I hate that term. Human intelligence is not generalized at all. Humans are highly specialized.
claims.source_title: Meta's Yann LeCun Asks How AIs will Match — and Exceed — Human-level Intelligence | Columbia Engineering
claims.cached_excerpt: I hate that term. Human intelligence is not generalized at all. Humans are highly specialized. All the problems we can fathom or imagine are problems we can fathom or imagine.

claims.citation: It may happen or happen to some degree within the next ten years.
claims.source_title: Yann LeCun: Human Intelligence is not General Intelligence // AI Inside 63
claims.cached_excerpt: It may happen or happen to some degree within the next ten years. We're going to make some conceptual advances, maybe based on JEPA, world models, planning, things like that, over the next few years.

claims.citation: The most optimistic view is that we'll have something that is close to human intelligence or maybe dog intelligence within five to 10 years.
claims.source_title: EP20: Yann LeCun
claims.cached_excerpt: The most optimistic view is that we'll have something that is close to human intelligence or maybe dog intelligence within five to 10 years. It's very likely that as what happened multiple times in the history of AI in the past, there's some obstacle we're not seeing yet, which will actually require us to invent some new conceptual things to go beyond.

claims.citation: There's a number of fallacies there. The first fallacy is that because a system is intelligent, it wants to take control. That's just completely false.

entity.name: Ed Markey
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Co-authored the AI Civil Rights Act requiring companies to conduct independent audits to identify algorithmic discrimination, prohibiting discriminatory AI use, and giving individuals right to appeal AI decisions to humans. Also introduced the Eliminating BIAS Act mandating federal agencies establish civil rights offices to combat algorithmic bias. Strongly opposed Trump administration's proposed 10-year moratorium on state AI regulation, leading bipartisan Senate efforts that defeated it 99-1. Advocates for 'moral leadership' in AI, stating 'we cannot abandon our principles in reckless pursuit of technological superiority.

claims.citation: Submitted as "5-10 years" via crowdsourced form (evidence: Explicitly stated)
claims.source_title: Mapping AI crowdsourced submission

claims.citation: Though AI promises immense benefits, it has already exposed its potential for harm and abuse. AI will only amplify that manipulation and exploitation.
claims.source_title: Oppenheimer Offers Us a Fresh Warning of AI's Danger | Scientific American
claims.cached_excerpt: Congress must act now to regulate tech companies to ensure that they prioritize the collective public interest. Congress should start by passing my Children's Online Privacy Protection Act, my Algorithmic Justice and Online Transparency Act and my bill prohibiting the launch of nuclear weapons by AI.

claims.citation: There is a real danger that we could have an AI revolution be unleashed without the safeguards that we know have to be put in place. [...] We're going to see discrimination in housing, in education, in applications for financing for mortgages, all across the board.
claims.source_title: Sen. Ed Markey on TikTok, the FCC, and the First Amendment | The Verge
claims.cached_excerpt: We know what the safeguards have to look like. We know what protections have to be built in, and they would not inhibit our ability to compete against the Chinese.

claims.citation: 'A 2019 report found that due in part to biased mortgage approval algorithms, lenders were 80% more likely to reject Black applicants than similar white applicants,' Markey said. 'A 2025 study found that AI resume screening tools almost never selected Black male names to proceed in the hiring process. This is unacceptable.'
claims.source_title: 'Unacceptable': Markey reintroduces legislation to combat bias in artificial intelligence | GBH
claims.cached_excerpt: The bill has what Markey describes as three key provisions. It would prohibit companies from offering, licensing, or using algorithms that discriminate on the basis of protected characteristics, like race or sex. It would require AI companies to conduct independent audits to identify and mitigate algorithmic discrimination.

claims.citation: It is the sense of Congress that— (1) the use of lethal, autonomous nuclear weapons systems that are not subject to meaningful human control cannot properly adhere to international humanitarian law; and (2) any decision to launch a nuclear weapon should not be made by artificial intelligence.
claims.source_title: Block Nuclear Launch by Autonomous AI Act
claims.cached_excerpt: Large-scale nuclear war would lead to the deaths of millions of people, firestorms, radioactive fallout contamination, agricultural failure, and catastrophic climate results.

claims.citation: To require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to carry out a study on the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, to require the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to convene a consortium on such environmental impacts, and to require the Director to develop a voluntary reporting system for the reporting of the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
claims.source_title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2024
claims.cached_excerpt: To require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to carry out a study on the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, to require the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to convene a consortium on such environmental impacts, and to require the Director to develop a voluntary reporting system for the reporting of the environmental impacts of artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.

claims.citation: Markey is sponsoring the Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act, which his office says would "put strict guardrails on companies' use of algorithms for consequential decisions, ensure algorithms are tested before and after deployment, help eliminate and prevent bias."
claims.source_title: Markey touts win as U.S. Senate scraps proposal to ban state AI regulation | WBUR News
claims.cached_excerpt: Massachusetts Sen. Ed Markey said in a statement that the vote "sent a clear message that Congress will not sell out our kids and local communities in order to pad the pockets of Big Tech billionaires."

claims.citation: "From developers to deployers, this legislation will place strict, strong, and unambiguous guardrails on the creation and use of artificial intelligence."
claims.source_title: Pressley, Clarke, Markey Reintroduce AI Civil Rights Act to Eliminate AI Discrimination
claims.cached_excerpt: Today, Congresswoman Ayanna Pressley (MA-07),alongside Senator Edward J. Markey (D-MA) and Representatives Yvette Clarke (NY-09), Pramila Jayapal (WA-07), and Summer Lee (PA-12), reintroduced their Artificial Intelligence (AI) Civil Rights Act, comprehensive legislation to prevent companies from using biased and discriminatory AI-powered algorithms to help make critical decisions in Americans' lives, ensure algorithms are tested before and after deployment, and increase transparency of complex algorithms used for critical decisions.

claims.citation: Markey introduced counter-legislation to block this order, emphasizing the need for state flexibility in addressing AI harms, including advertising abuses.
claims.source_title: Senator Markey Urges AI Regulations to Combat Hidden Ads in Chatbots
claims.cached_excerpt: Markey introduced counter-legislation to block this order, emphasizing the need for state flexibility in addressing AI harms, including advertising abuses.

entity.name: Future of Life Institute
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Advocates for mandatory safety standards, pre-deployment licensing, and audits for advanced AI systems. Calls for moratoriums on uncontrollable AI development, export controls on frontier models, and strict liability frameworks. Supports tiered regulatory approach with increasingly stringent requirements based on AI capabilities.
entity.belief_threat_models: Loss of control, Existential risk, Power concentration

claims.citation: FLI believes that frontier AI is currently being developed in an unsafe, unaccountable, and ungoverned manner en route to smarter-than-human general-purpose AI (a.k.a. artificial superintelligence or superhuman AGI).
claims.source_title: Our Position on AI - Future of Life Institute
claims.cached_excerpt: FLI advocates for effective and meaningful governance of AI, including the implementation of robust licensing, auditing, oversight and liability regimes.

claims.citation: Although AGI stands for "Artificial General Intelligence", it is a useful mnemonic to think of it as an "Autonomous General Intelligence": the triple combination of Autonomy (independence of action), Generality (breath of capability, application, and learning), and Intelligence (competence at tasks).
claims.source_title: Safety Standards Delivering Controllable and Beneficial AI Tools - Future of Life Institute
claims.cached_excerpt: Although AGI stands for "Artificial General Intelligence", it is a useful mnemonic to think of it as an "Autonomous General Intelligence": the triple combination of Autonomy (independence of action), Generality (breath of capability, application, and learning), and Intelligence (competence at tasks).

claims.citation: The product of an intelligence explosion would be not just Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) – a system about as capable as a human across a wide range of domains – but a superintelligence, a system that far surpasses our cognitive abilities.
claims.source_title: Are we close to an intelligence explosion? - Future of Life Institute
claims.cached_excerpt: Timelines to superintelligent AI vary widely, from just two or three years(a common prediction by lab insiders) to a couple of decades (the median guess among the broader machine learning community).

claims.citation: Through misalignment or misuse, this represents an existential threat to humanity, either through terminal disempowerment or literal extinction.
claims.source_title: Our Position on AI - Future of Life Institute
claims.cached_excerpt: FLI advocates for effective and meaningful governance of AI, including the implementation of robust licensing, auditing, oversight and liability regimes.

claims.stance: Strongly supports compute-based reporting and thresholds
claims.citation: We recommend that BIS leverage this provision to create a registry for large aggregations of advanced chips (or 'computing clusters'), including registering the individual Graphic Processing Units (GPUs) used within these clusters, within the United States. Creating such a registry would provide BIS with a clear picture of the distributed computational capacity across the country, enabling for more proactive monitoring of potential dual-use foundation model development.
claims.source_title: Input on Federal AI Reporting Requirements
claims.cached_excerpt: We recommend that BIS leverage this provision to create a registry for large aggregations of advanced chips (or 'computing clusters'), including registering the individual Graphic Processing Units (GPUs) used within these clusters, within the United States. Creating such a registry would provide BIS with a clear picture of the distributed computational capacity across the country, enabling for more proactive monitoring of potential dual-use foundation model development.

claims.citation: Export controls, especially those with a wide and expansive purview, are likely to suffer from serious gaps in enforcement. A growing informal economy around chip smuggling has already emerged over the last few years, and it is likely to grow as BIS rules become more expansive. A solution focused on hardware governance is less liable to this gap in enforcement.
claims.source_title: FLI Response to BIS: Request for Comments on Implementation of Additional Export Controls - Future of Life Institute
claims.cached_excerpt: Export controls, especially those with a wide and expansive purview, are likely to suffer from serious gaps in enforcement. A growing informal economy around chip smuggling has already emerged over the last few years, and it is likely to grow as BIS rules become more expansive. A solution focused on hardware governance is less liable to this gap in enforcement.

claims.citation: The Future of Life Institute is founded by our board members and holds its launch event at MIT.
claims.cached_excerpt: The Future of Life Institute is founded by our board members and holds its launch event at MIT.

claims.citation: To provide the necessary financial incentives for profit-driven AI developers to exercise abundant caution, we recommend the urgent adoption of a framework for liability for AI-derived harms. At a minimum, this framework should hold developers of general-purpose AI systems and AI systems likely to be deployed for critical functions strictly liable for resulting harms to individuals, property, communities, and society.
claims.source_title: Policymaking in the Pause - Future of Life Institute
claims.cached_excerpt: To ensure proper vetting of powerful AI systems before deployment, we recommend a robust independent auditing regime for models that are general-purpose, trained on large amounts of compute, or intended for use in circumstances likely to impact the rights or the wellbeing of individuals, communities, or society. This mandatory third-party auditing and certification scheme could be derived from the EU's proposed 'conformity assessments' and should be adopted by jurisdictions worldwide.

claims.stance: Supports restrictions on frontier open weights
claims.citation: For highly capable or impactful open model GPAI, risks are amplified by the inability of the original provider to effectively remediate or recall a dangerous open model after it has been released and downloaded innumerable times. While their inherent transparency may have benefits for accountability, it can also provide malicious actors with access to the model weights, enabling the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities or the circumvention of guardrails.
claims.source_title: FLI Response to EU Commission Competition Inquiry
claims.cached_excerpt: For highly capable or impactful open model GPAI, risks are amplified by the inability of the original provider to effectively remediate or recall a dangerous open model after it has been released and downloaded innumerable times. claims.citation: HISTORICAL (March 2023): Therefore, we call on all AI labs to immediately pause for at least 6 months the training of AI systems more powerful than GPT-4. 27,565 verified signatures by May 5, 2023. Current FLI stance (2025-2026) has evolved to prohibition on superintelligence development and commonsense regulation.

claims.citation: "The fragmentation of the risk literature should give us pause. We are potentially in a situation where many may believe they've grasped the full picture after consulting one or two sources, when in reality they're navigating AI with significant blind spots."

claims.citation: "Several speakers discussed shortcomings of existing models and the risks posed by more advanced AI. They suggested that we should be carefully preparing for risks and potentially slowing down particularly risky development and deployment."

claims.citation: "We had a very interesting discussion exploring our different perspectives on AI timelines. We didn't have much time to get into details during the short discussion, but the high level summary was that many people in FutureTech were more pessimistic about a few aspects of the scenario. They expect that AI will be slower and less effective at rapidly speeding up AI research and have slower and lower adoption in key sectors and across the economy as a whole."

entity.name: Roman Yampolskiy
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Strongly advocates for extreme caution in AI development, arguing that superintelligent AI cannot be indefinitely controlled. Promotes narrow AI development over AGI and calls for potential moratoriums on superintelligence research until safety is better understood.
entity.belief_threat_models: Existential risk, Loss of control, Cybersecurity

claims.citation: Artificial general intelligence was a system capable of performing in any domain a human could perform. So you're creating this average artificial person. They can do cognitive labor, physical labor where you can get another human to do it.

claims.cached_excerpt: The prediction markets right now are saying 2026 for AGI. I heard the same thing from CEO of Anthropic DeepMind. So maybe we're two years away, which seems very soon given we don't have a working safety mechanism in place or even a prototype for one.

claims.citation: So the definitions we used to have, and people are modifying them a little bit lately, artificial general intelligence was a system capable of performing in any domain a human could perform. entity.name: Machine Intelligence Research Institute (MIRI)
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Advocates for international agreements to halt progress toward smarter-than-human AI until alignment problems are solved. Believes current technical understanding is vastly insufficient and calls for indefinite worldwide moratorium on large training runs, enforced by international agreement. Has shifted from technical research to policy advocacy believing technical solutions won't arrive in time.
entity.belief_threat_models: Loss of control, Existential risk, Power concentration

claims.citation: The default trajectory of AI development has an unacceptably high likelihood of leading to human extinction. Critical research and policy is needed to course correct.
claims.source_title: AI Governance to Avoid Extinction

claims.citation: Our objective is to convince major powers to shut down the development of frontier AI systems worldwide before it is too late. We believe that nothing less than this will prevent future misaligned smarter-than-human AI systems from destroying humanity.
claims.source_title: MIRI 2024 Communications Strategy - Machine Intelligence Research Institute

claims.citation: In 2000, Eliezer Yudkowsky founded the Singularity Institute for Artificial Intelligence with funding from Brian and Sabine Atkins

In contrast, humans have problem-solving abilities that allow us to adapt to new contexts and excel in many domains other than what the ancestral environment prepared us for. If machines can achieve human equivalence in cognitive tasks, then it is very likely that they can eventually outperform humans.
claims.source_title: FAQ

claims.citation: It is this general problem-solving ability that we have in mind when we talk about 'artificial general intelligence' (AGI) or 'smarter-than-human AI.' AI systems may come to surpass humans in science and engineering abilities without being particularly human-like in any other respects — artificial intelligence need not imply artificial consciousness, for example, or artificial emotions. Instead, we have in mind the capacity to model real-world environments well and identify a variety of ways to put those environments into new states.
claims.source_title: Why AI Safety? - Machine Intelligence Research Institute

claims.citation: The stated goal of the world's leading AI companies is to build AI that is general enough to do anything a human can do, from solving hard problems in theoretical physics to deftly navigating social environments. Recent machine learning progress seems to have brought this goal within reach. At this point, we would be uncomfortable ruling out the possibility that AI more capable than any human is achieved in the next year or two, and we would be moderately surprised if this outcome were still two decades away.
claims.source_title: The Problem

claims.citation: In a fall 2023 poll of most MIRI researchers, we expect AGI (according to the definition from this Metaculus market) in a median of 9 years and a mean of 14.6 years. One researcher was an outlier at 52 years; the majority predicted under ten years.
claims.source_title: MIRI 2024 Mission and Strategy Update

claims.citation: Recent progress has been sufficiently rapid that we at MIRI would not be surprised if ASI is developed in two or five years, and we would be surprised if it were still more than twenty years away.
claims.source_title: Why expect smarter-than-human AI to be developed any time soon?

claims.citation: Our primary concern is mitigating catastrophic and extinction risks associated with artificial intelligence systems as they approach and surpass human capabilities.
claims.source_title: AI Governance to Avoid Extinction

claims.citation: The default consequence of the creation of artificial superintelligence (ASI) is human extinction.
claims.source_title: The Machine Intelligence Research Institute (MIRI)

claims.citation: If we were to put a number on how likely extinction is in the absence of an aggressive near-term policy response, MIRI's research leadership would give one upward of 90%.
claims.source_title: The Problem

claims.citation: The only sufficient response to the current situation, in which smarter-than-human AI is imminent despite an immature scientific field, is a global halt. The current path, along with the most likely variations, pose unacceptably large risks, including terrorism, great power war, lock-in of bad values, and loss of control due to misaligned systems.
claims.source_title: Summary: AI Governance to Avoid Extinction - Machine Intelligence Research Institute

claims.citation: Policy: Increase the probability that the major governments of the world end up coming to some international agreement to halt progress toward smarter-than-human AI, until humanity's state of knowledge and justified confidence about its understanding of relevant phenomena has drastically changed; and until we are able to secure these systems such that they can't fall into the hands of malicious or incautious actors.
claims.source_title: MIRI 2024 Mission and Strategy Update

entity.name: 80,000 Hours
entity.belief_regulatory_stance_detail: Advocates for careful development of AI systems with strong safety measures. Promotes working on AI governance, technical safety research, and policy approaches to reduce catastrophic risks. Estimates ~10% existential risk from AI within 100 years and calls it 'the world's most pressing problem'.
entity.belief_threat_models: Existential risk, Loss of control, Power concentration

claims.citation: 80,000 Hours is a company based in Oxford (United Kingdom) founded in 2011.
Typically, when people say 'AGI' what they have in mind is something like 'at least human level or better at most cognitive tasks.' This is roughly what I'll take it to mean in this article.

claims.citation: Typically, when people say 'AGI' what they have in mind is something like 'at least human level or better at most cognitive tasks.' This is roughly what I'll take it to mean in this article.

claims.source_title: Will we have AGI by 2030? | 80,000 Hours

claims.citation: The chance of an AGI-driven technological explosion before 2030 — creating one of the most pivotal periods in history — is high enough to act on.

claims.citation: This transformation could bring enormous benefits, helping us solve currently intractable global problems. But it could also pose severe risks, some of which could be existential— meaning they could cause human extinction, or an equally permanent and severe disempowerment of humanity.
claims.source_title: Why AI risks are the world's most pressing problems | 80,000 Hours

claims.citation: Preventing future power-seeking AIs from disempowering humanity is one of the most pressing problems of our time to work on. The window for developing effective safeguards may be narrow, and the stakes are extremely high.
claims.source_title: Risks from power-seeking AI systems | 80,000 Hours

claims.citation: Public policy and corporate governance in particular may be necessary to ensure that advanced AI is broadly beneficial and low risk.
claims.source_title: AI governance and policy | Career review | 80,000 Hours

claims.citation: At 80,000 Hours, we think a significant number of people should build expertise to work on United States policy relevant to the long-term effects and potentially catastrophic risks of the development and use of artificial intelligence.
claims.source_title: Working in US AI policy

claims.citation: But we need a range of strategies for risk reduction. Public policy and corporate governance in particular may be necessary to ensure that advanced AI is broadly beneficial and low risk.
claims.source_title: AI governance and policy | Career review - 80000 Hours

claims.citation: METR assessed whether OpenAI GPT-5 could pose catastrophic risks, considering three key threat models: AI R&D Automation: AI systems speeding up AI researchers by >10X, Rogue replication: AI systems posing direct risks of rapid takeover, and Strategic sabotage.

claims.citation: We are currently developing evaluations for AI R&D capabilities that may serve as 'red lines', as well as a continuous measure of autonomous capabilities in general.

claims.citation: We think there's a lot of potential in evals-based AI rules and norms: requirements that AI systems be evaluated for dangerous capabilities, and restricted when that's the only way to contain the risks.

claims.source_title: How the Trump administration changed AI: A timeline | ZDNET
claims.cached_excerpt: The administration's record thus far has emphasized progress and investment with little regard for safety or responsibility... the forthcoming 20-page plan focuses on 'promoting innovation, reducing regulatory burdens and overhauling permitting,' avoiding highly contested topics like copyright in training data.

claims.citation: The framework argues that the government's job is not to regulate the development of the technology but to remove barriers to deployment.
claims.source_title: The Trump Administration's AI Policy Framework Has an Ideology. It Just Won't Admit It.
claims.cached_excerpt: The framework argues that the government's job is not to regulate the development of the technology but to remove barriers to deployment.

claims.citation: The Framework reflects a policy preference for a sector-specific, federally led regulatory model with significant preemption of state AI laws. It builds on the December 2025 Executive Order (EO) on AI, which directed the administration to develop legislative recommendations for Congress while deploying executive tools, including an AI Litigation Task Force, to identify and, where appropriate, challenge state AI laws viewed as inconsistent with federal policy in the interim.
claims.source_title: Trump Administration Releases National AI Policy Framework
claims.cached_excerpt: The Framework recommends that Congress preempt state AI laws that 'impose undue burdens,' while preserving state authority in certain areas, including traditional police powers to protect children and prevent fraud, state zoning authority over AI infrastructure, and requirements governing a state's own use of AI in procurement and public services.

claims.citation: The Administration is calling on Congress to take steps to remove outdated or unnecessary barriers to innovation, accelerate the deployment of AI across industry sectors, and facilitate broad access to the testing environments needed to build and deploy world-class AI systems.
claims.source_title: President Donald J. Trump Unveils National AI Legislative Framework – The White House
claims.cached_excerpt: The Administration is calling on Congress to take steps to remove outdated or unnecessary barriers to innovation, accelerate the deployment of AI across industry sectors, and facilitate broad access to the testing environments needed to build and deploy world-class AI systems.

claims.citation: President Trump: 'Investment in AI is helping to make the U.S. Economy the "HOTTEST" in the World, but overregulation by the States is threatening to undermine this Major Growth "Engine." ... We MUST have one Federal Standard instead of a patchwork of 50 State Regulatory Regimes. If we don't, then China will easily catch us in the AI race.'
claims.source_title: Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Ensures a National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence – The White House
claims.cached_excerpt: ENABLING COMMON SENSE AI POLICY: The Trump Administration supports a common sense approach to AI policy. This means removing unnecessary red tape, updating out-of-date rules created before AI advancements, and clarifying new rules for certain sectors or use-cases.
